_description: Five-song trial corpus assembled 2026-06-18. Seed of the calibration corpus described in the 'engine learning' design note. Each entry pairs a YouTube URL (the audio source the JAM UI consumes via /api/analyze-url) with the Songsterr tab PDF used as ground truth for per-section guidance-mode labels. Tab PDFs live outside the repo under /Users/mattharvey/Downloads/ because they are copyrighted; reproducing them locally requires re-downloading from Songsterr. The structure is intentionally machine-readable so a future sweep script (see TODO 'Write up findings + propose engine fixes (incl. calibration corpus)') can ingest it.
_blocked_on: Stem separation must run for the guidance-mode classifier to do useful work. Engine-fix shipped 2026-06-18 enables separate_stems=True on PipelineConfig.standard() so the 'studio' analysis_mode (the JAM UI default) actually produces 4 stems for Demucs to feed into the multi-stem voter.
_rerun_command: for song in songs: curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/analyze-url -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"url":<url>,"source_kind":"auto","platform":"auto","fast_mode":false,"analysis_mode":"studio","extract_midi":true,"use_local_engine":false}'

songs.first_trial_failure_mode: NOT YET ANALYZED — held until stem separation is enabled. CRITICAL EDGE CASE: solo polyphonic guitar means even with Demucs we get one 'other' stem; the chord/riff/lead trichotomy needs a fourth 'fingerstyle' branch or a sub-window classifier.

songs.title: Sex On Fire
songs.tab_pdf: /Users/mattharvey/Downloads/Sex On Fire Solo Tab by Kings Of Leon | Songsterr Tabs with Rhythm.pdf

tab_facts.engine_detected_key_note: G# minor is the relative minor of E major (same 4-sharp key signature). Classic tonal-template ambiguity: chroma-correlation can match the major key or its relative minor unless tonic emphasis is weighed. Surface as a known key-detection failure mode in the audit.
tab_facts.lead_riff_motif: single-string palm-muted monophonic figure (fret pattern 4-4-4-7-6-4) with let-ring, played only during Verse 1 (bars 22-33) and Verse 2 riff section (bars 46-55)

ground_truth_sections.label: Intro
ground_truth_sections.notes: bars 1-21 (~0.0-33.0s). Lead guitar tacet. Rhythm guitar plays the anthemic open-chord wash.

ground_truth_sections.label: Verse 1 riff
ground_truth_sections.notes: bars 22-33 (~33.0-51.8s). Lead plays the signature single-string palm-muted monophonic riff (fret pattern 4-4-4-7-6-4) with let-ring — the song's hook.

ground_truth_sections.label: Chorus 1 / Verse 2a
ground_truth_sections.notes: bars 34-45 (~51.8-70.6s). Lead tacet; rhythm chord wash continues.

ground_truth_sections.label: Verse 2 riff
ground_truth_sections.notes: bars 46-55 (~70.6-86.3s). Lead returns to the single-string palm-muted riff motif (fret pattern 4-4-4-7-6-4).

ground_truth_sections.label: Chorus 2 + Bridge + Outro
ground_truth_sections.notes: bars 56-130 (~86.3-204.0s). Lead tacet across the rest of the song; ends on the chord wash.

songs.first_trial_failure_mode: Run A bundle b640c78a (post-Demucs-determinism fix, 2026-06-20): 20 engine-segments, ~14/20 coarse-mode correct. Failure pattern: (a) Verse 1 riff segments (engine #5, #6, #7 spanning ~33-67s) all classified chord/conf 0.68-1.00 — riff not detected on the iconic single-string palm-muted motif; (b) Verse 2 riff segments (engine #8, #9 spanning ~67-110s) similar false-negative chord classification; (c) one false-positive lead at engine #18 (~176s, outro chord wash) classified lead/conf 0.77. Net: classifier under-fires on riff and occasionally over-fires on lead. Suggests monophonic_ratio + repetition signals are not crossing thresholds — possibly because the riff is heavily P.M.'d (short envelopes shorten note durations and may starve voiced_frame_ratio), or because Demucs splits the lead guitar's riff into other/guitar in a way that dilutes the per-stem mono signal.
